name: Chilled Ehu & Molokai Sweet Potato Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Molokai sweet potatoes (pared and diced)
Salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch ehu in 2 cups of the fish stock; remove, dice, and chill.
2. In a saucepot, heat margarine; sauté leeks.
3. Add wine, sweet potatoes, limu, and the remaining 4 cups of fish stock to the saucepot. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer for 30 minutes or until sweet potatoes are soft.
4. Add salt and white pepper to taste.
5. Puree the soup in a blender.
6. Chill the pureed soup.
7. Before serving, add milk to the desired consistency.
8. Divide the chilled ehu between two serving bowls. Add the cooled soup to each bowl.
To make fish stock: Clean 3 lb of fish heads and bones under running water. Place them in a saucepot with 1 cup white wine, 1/2 cup diced onion, 1/2 cup diced leeks, 1/2 cup diced celery, 2 sprigs fresh thyme, and 3 quarts of water. Cover and bring to a boil. Skim the surface and simmer for 15 minutes. Strain the stock through a fine sieve. Makes 2 quarts.
Courtesy of Tim Le, Sheraton Waikiki
